codes.title: Project Mirror Labyrinth Codes
codes.intro: Redeem active Project Mirror Labyrinth codes for Fragments, Condense, Seeds of Light, and Reflection-related rewards. Codes are case-sensitive and usually require liking the Roblox experience and joining the official community before they will redeem.
codes.howTo: Launch Project Mirror Labyrinth, open the Codes menu from the left side of the screen, enter the code exactly as written, then press Confirm to claim the reward.

activeCodes.note: Best used toward Reflection and Outfit progression.
A larger Fragment reward for early account progression.
Enter the code with the exact capitalization shown.
Useful for saving toward Outfit pulls and related upgrades.
The starter code highlighted on the Roblox game page.

tips: Like the Roblox experience and join the official community before entering codes.
Enter each code with the exact capitalization shown, because Project Mirror Labyrinth codes are case-sensitive.
If a valid code is rejected, rejoin a fresh server after an update and try again.
Redeem active codes early, because update cycles can rotate older rewards out.

beginnerGuide.title: How to Start Project Mirror Labyrinth
beginnerGuide.intro: Project Mirror Labyrinth is a Roblox roguelite deck builder inspired by Slay the Spire, Library of Ruina, and Limbus Company. New players should focus on starting dungeon runs, learning card combat, and using free code rewards before chasing deeper progression.

steps.title: Join the Roblox experience
steps.description: Open Project Mirror Labyrinth from its Roblox game page and launch the experience. It is listed as a roguelite deck builder with heavy inspiration from card-based strategy games.
steps.tip: Controller support is not listed on the Roblox page, so play with keyboard and mouse.

steps.title: Like the game and join the official community
steps.description: Liking the experience and joining the official Project Mirror Labyrinth Roblox community are required for code redemption on current code guides.
steps.tip: Do this before entering codes so the redemption menu does not reject valid rewards.

steps.title: Redeem the starter code
steps.description: Use the code Welcome to claim starter rewards including Fragments, Condense, and Seeds of Light.
steps.tip: Enter codes exactly as written because Project Mirror Labyrinth codes are case-sensitive.

steps.title: Start your first dungeon
steps.description: Press Tab and then Play, or interact with the NO ENTRY gate, to begin a dungeon run.
steps.tip: Dungeon runs are where the deck-building loop starts.

steps.title: Learn attack and defense cards
steps.description: Combat uses cards for attacks and defense. Your goal is to build a working deck combo and play it carefully to survive maze-like stages.
steps.tip: Early runs are for learning enemy patterns, card timing, and which defensive tools keep you alive.

steps.title: Spend rewards on progression systems
steps.description: Fragments are tied to Reflections, Outfits, skills, and broader account progression. Tickets and rarer materials support stronger pulls or Outfit growth.
steps.tip: Save high-value ticket rewards for Reflection-related progression instead of spending them randomly.

steps.title: Check weekly update timing
steps.description: The Roblox page lists maintenance around 2:00 P.M. every Tuesday.
steps.tip: If the game is unavailable around that time, maintenance is the first thing to check.

gameplayMechanics.title: Project Mirror Labyrinth Gameplay Mechanics
gameplayMechanics.intro: Project Mirror Labyrinth combines roguelite dungeon runs with deck-building card combat. The core gameplay is about entering dungeons, building useful card combinations, and using attack and defense cards to survive each run.

items.meaning: Runs are built around repeatable dungeon attempts and deck decisions rather than a single fixed combat loadout.
items.action: Start dungeon runs, adapt your deck, and improve through repeated attempts.
items.bestFor: Players who like strategic retries, card choices, and gradual mastery.

items.meaning: Two ways to start a dungeon: press Tab and then Play, or interact with the NO ENTRY gate.
items.action: Use either method from the lobby to begin a run.
items.bestFor: New players finding the actual start point.

items.meaning: Attack cards are part of the combat deck and are used to deal damage during encounters.
items.action: Build a deck that can reliably damage enemies before a run falls apart.
items.bestFor: Offensive builds and faster dungeon clears.

items.meaning: Defense cards are used to survive enemy pressure and reduce the risk of losing a run early.
items.action: Balance damage cards with enough defensive tools to survive harder encounters.
items.bestFor: Stable beginner runs and safer progression.

items.meaning: The game rewards smart card combinations rather than random play.
items.action: Look for cards that support each other across damage, defense, and resource use.
items.bestFor: Players pushing deeper into maze-like stages.

items.meaning: Fragments are used to draw Outfits from Targeted and Standard Reflections, and Outfits are tied to stats and passive growth.
items.action: Collect Fragments and tickets, then use them to expand progression options.
items.bestFor: Long-term account growth and build variety.

items.meaning: Condense, Seeds of Light, and other currencies are connected to Defragmenting Outfits and unlocking passive abilities.
items.action: Use rarer materials on Outfit growth after securing useful Reflection pulls.
items.bestFor: Improving passive bonuses and strengthening future dungeon runs.

items.meaning: The Roblox page lists maintenance around 2:00 P.M. every Tuesday.
items.action: Check update timing before assuming the game is broken.

reflectionsAndOutfits.title: Project Mirror Labyrinth Reflections and Outfits
reflectionsAndOutfits.intro: Reflections, Outfits, Fragments, tickets, Condense, and Seeds of Light are central to Project Mirror Labyrinth progression. This grid explains what each system is used for based on confirmed mechanics.

items.name: Targeted Reflections
items.use: Used to draw Outfits with a more focused Reflection target.
items.focus: Spend Fragments here when chasing specific Outfit progression.

items.name: Standard Reflections
items.use: Used to draw Outfits through the standard Reflection pool.
items.focus: Use as the general Outfit acquisition route when no targeted option is the main goal.

items.name: Fragments
items.use: Required for drawing Outfits from Targeted and Standard Reflections and tied to learning skills and improving dungeon runs.
items.focus: Redeem active codes first, then save Fragments for Reflection and Outfit progression.

items.name: Mega Reflection Ticket
items.use: A ticket reward connected to Reflection pulls.
items.focus: Treat as a high-value pull resource rather than ordinary currency.

items.name: SR Guaranteed Mega Ticket
items.use: A stronger Reflection ticket reward that guarantees SR value from the Mega Reflection system.

items.name: Outfits
items.use: Outfits gained through Reflections grant new stats and contribute to build growth.
items.focus: Build around Outfits that support your preferred dungeon strategy.

items.name: Defragment
items.use: Defragmenting Outfits unlocks passive abilities and uses materials beyond ordinary Fragments.
items.focus: Prioritize Defragmenting once you have an Outfit worth improving.

items.name: Condense
items.use: A material connected to Defragmenting and passive ability progression.
items.focus: Hold for Outfit upgrades instead of spending casually.

items.name: Seeds of Light
items.use: A material used alongside other currencies for Outfit growth and passive unlocks.
items.focus: Keep starter code rewards for Defragment-related progression.

currenciesAndRewards.title: Currencies and Rewards in Project Mirror Labyrinth
currenciesAndRewards.intro: Project Mirror Labyrinth rewards are built around Fragments first, with Condense, Seeds of Light, and Tickets appearing as higher-value extras. Codes are one of the fastest ways to collect these resources before pushing deeper into dungeon runs.

items.mainUse: Used for buying Reflections, drawing Outfits, learning skills, and strengthening dungeon runs.
Used in the Outfit Defragmenting path, which connects to passive bonus unlocks.
Used with higher-value progression rewards tied to Defragmenting Outfits and passive growth.
Used as a Reflection pull voucher, saving Fragments when drawing from Reflection systems.
examples: EGGRNGFORBRAINROTS: 1 Mega Reflection Ticket and 910 Fragments

items.mainUse: Used for a higher-value Reflection pull with an SR guarantee.
Used as a special Ticket reward connected to stronger EGO-related progression options.

dungeonGuide.title: How to Start a Dungeon in Project Mirror Labyrinth
dungeonGuide.intro: Project Mirror Labyrinth gives players two clear dungeon entry paths. The official Roblox page lists the TAB menu route and the NO ENTRY gate route.

steps.title: Launch Project Mirror Labyrinth
steps.description: Open the Roblox experience page and enter the game. Controller support is not available, so use keyboard and mouse.

steps.title: Press TAB
steps.description: Press TAB to open the in-game menu, then choose Play to start a dungeon.

steps.title: Use the NO ENTRY Gate
steps.description: As an alternate route, interact with the NO ENTRY gate in the game world to begin a dungeon.

steps.title: Build Through the Run
steps.description: Project Mirror Labyrinth is a roguelite deck builder, so each run focuses on improving card options, managing encounters, and building stronger combinations.

steps.title: Check Weekly Maintenance
steps.description: Maintenance begins around 2:00 P.M. every Tuesday. Dungeon access, server availability, and UI behavior can be affected during update windows.

steps.title: Rejoin After Updates
steps.description: When a menu, code, or entry path does not respond after a new update, leave the experience and rejoin a refreshed server.

updatesAndMaintenance.title: Project Mirror Labyrinth Updates, Codes, and Maintenance
updatesAndMaintenance.intro: Project Mirror Labyrinth changes visible update labels through its Roblox title and rotates code rewards around patches, milestones, and content drops. Weekly maintenance is also part of the normal rhythm.

items.label: Roblox Title Tag
items.title: [LEI HENG + NOT BINAH]
items.description: The Roblox experience title currently uses this update tag, making it the first place to check for the active content theme.
items.impact: Use the title tag to identify whether you are looking at the newest game version and code cycle.

items.label: Weekly Maintenance
items.title: Tuesday Maintenance Window
items.description: The official Roblox description says maintenance begins around 2:00 P.M. every Tuesday.
items.impact: Dungeon entry, server access, and reward redemption can be disrupted around maintenance.

items.label: Code Rotation
items.title: Active Reward Codes
items.description: Recent public code lists include UrbanPlague, OfficeDelay, NepoOffice, FauvistStudents, and Welcome as reward sources.
items.impact: Redeem working codes before update cycles remove or replace them.

items.label: Official Community
items.title: Roblox Community Updates
items.description: The official Roblox community group is the main hub for updates, code requirements, and game announcements.
items.impact: Joining the community is also required for redeeming codes.

items.label: Server State
items.title: Rejoin for Fresh Servers
items.description: After new updates, restarting the experience can move players to a newer server where menus and rewards behave correctly.
items.impact: Use this first when a fresh code or menu option does not work.

storyAndInspiration.title: Story, Lore, and Inspirations Behind Project Mirror Labyrinth
storyAndInspiration.intro: Project Mirror Labyrinth is a Roblox roguelite deck builder made by its own creator community. It draws heavy inspiration from Project Moon-style worlds and card-battle games while remaining a standalone Roblox experience.

faqs.question: What kind of game is Project Mirror Labyrinth?
faqs.answer: Project Mirror Labyrinth is a Roblox roguelite deck builder. Runs focus on card-based choices, dungeon progression, and building stronger options over repeated attempts.

faqs.question: Which games inspired Project Mirror Labyrinth?
faqs.answer: The official Roblox description names Slay the Spire, Library of Ruina, and Limbus Company as major gameplay and style inspirations for Project Mirror Labyrinth.

faqs.question: Is Project Mirror Labyrinth an official Project Moon game?
faqs.answer: No. Project Mirror Labyrinth is created by its own community group and is described around Project Moon-inspired themes, not as a Project Moon official release.

faqs.question: Where does the Project Mirror Labyrinth story take place?
faqs.answer: The Roblox page places the Project Mirror Labyrinth story shortly after the events of Angela's Bad End in Library of Ruina.

faqs.question: Does Project Mirror Labyrinth spoil Library of Ruina?
faqs.answer: The official Roblox description states that Project Mirror Labyrinth contains no major spoilers.

faqs.question: Do players need to play Library of Ruina before Project Mirror Labyrinth?
faqs.answer: No. The game page states that players do not have to play Library of Ruina to understand Project Mirror Labyrinth.

faq.title: Project Mirror Labyrinth
faq.subtitle: Quick answers about Project Mirror Labyrinth codes, dungeons, and progression

questions.question: What is Project Mirror Labyrinth?
questions.answer: Project Mirror Labyrinth is a Roblox roguelite deck builder inspired by Slay the Spire, Library of Ruina, and Limbus Company, set in a Project Moon-inspired world.

questions.question: How do I redeem codes in Project Mirror Labyrinth?
questions.answer: Launch Project Mirror Labyrinth, open the Codes menu from the left side of the screen, enter the code exactly as written (codes are case-sensitive), and press Confirm to claim the reward.

questions.question: Where do I start a dungeon in Project Mirror Labyrinth?
questions.answer: In Project Mirror Labyrinth you can press TAB then Play, or interact with the NO ENTRY gate, to begin a dungeon run.

questions.question: What are Fragments used for in Project Mirror Labyrinth?
questions.answer: In Project Mirror Labyrinth, Fragments are used to draw Outfits from Targeted and Standard Reflections, learn skills, and strengthen dungeon runs.

questions.question: When is Project Mirror Labyrinth maintenance?
questions.answer: Project Mirror Labyrinth maintenance begins around 2:00 P.M. every Tuesday, which can affect dungeon entry and code redemption.

questions.question: Is Project Mirror Labyrinth an official Project Moon game?
questions.answer: No. Project Mirror Labyrinth is a Roblox experience created by its own community group, inspired by Project Moon-style worlds rather than being an official Project Moon release.
